prediction.reasoning: Predicting stock prices is highly speculative and subject to numerous unpredictable factors. This forecast is generated using a simplified statistical model (geometric Brownian motion) that assumes future price movements follow a log-normal distribution. The point estimate is set to the current price ($163.48) reflecting the inherent unpredictability and lack of specific predictive insights over a 20-day timeframe without further market data or analysis. The confidence intervals are calculated based on an estimated annualized volatility of 30%. This model does not incorporate real-time market news, company-specific developments, fundamental analysis, technical indicators, or broader economic factors, all of which can significantly influence actual stock performance. Therefore, these intervals are theoretical and actual results may fall outside these ranges.
